data.task:
Ticket 0050 atom 1 of ~3: reconcile the archive-confirmation RUNTIME SEMANTICS in packages/core and lock them with tests. The archive-confirmation stack is already implemented (uncommitted) across runner.ts/status.ts/types.ts/launcher.ts/routes.ts, but it left the core test suite RED (4 failing tests in packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts) and the disposition/status derivation is internally inconsistent. Fix ONLY packages/core this atom.

WRITE-SCOPE (touch nothing else): packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts, packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts, packages/core/tests/status.test.ts. Do NOT touch packages/daemon, launcher.ts, routes.ts, or packages/personas/** this atom.

REQUIRED CHANGES:

1) deriveWrapDisposition (runner.ts ~line 424): make founder-decision take precedence over archive-ready, drop the obsolete build-atom/signal gating, and remove the now-dead archive-candidate branch. Final logic must be exactly this precedence:
   - if founderDecisionNeeded(...) -> 'awaiting-founder'
   - else if target === 'priority' && closeoutRunStatus === 'archive ready' -> 'archive-confirmation'
   - else -> 'continue'
   Remove the unreachable archive-candidate return (current line ~428). If builderDispatchCount becomes unused, drop the param and update its call site + any direct test callers; otherwise leave it.

2) deriveWrapupRunStatus (runner.ts ~line 402): make founder-decision take precedence over archive-ready for priority target too. Final priority-branch precedence: if founderDecisionNeeded(...) -> 'awaiting-founder'; else if runStatus === 'archive ready' -> 'awaiting-archive-confirmation'; else return current. (Leave the ticket branch unchanged.)

3) Remove 'archive-candidate' from the WrapDisposition union (runner.ts:90) since it is no longer produced. Grep the whole tree for 'archive-candidate' and update every remaining reference (including the sample disposition string in packages/core/tests/status.test.ts ~line 109/113 -> use 'archive-confirmation').

4) Migrate/extend the archive tests in packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts to the new semantics. Expected behavior after the change:
   - archive-ready wrap, NO other founder decision, ZERO build atoms -> wrap-disposition event disposition='archive-confirmation', result.status='awaiting-archive-confirmation', and the event data must carry an 'action' object {type:'archive-priority-confirmation', endpoint:'/runs/<runId>/archive-confirmation', confirmWith:'archive', priorityId, runId, workspaceId}.
   - archive-ready wrap, NO other founder decision, WITH >=1 build atom (the run_224 case) -> SAME result: disposition='archive-confirmation', status='awaiting-archive-confirmation', action present. (This is the regression the ticket is about: build atoms must NOT downgrade it.)
   - archive-ready wrap that ALSO has a real Founder Decision Needed (not None) -> disposition='awaiting-founder', status='awaiting-founder', NO action (founder decision wins). Keep this as an explicit regression guard.
   - The existing 'without a runnable signal -> continue' test premise is obsolete (signal no longer gates); repurpose it to assert that an archive-ready wrap with NO cited signal still records archive-confirmation (proves signal-independence), rather than deleting the coverage.
   Rename the migrated tests so their names match the new behavior (no stale 'archive-candidate'/'continue' names).

5) ADD a parsing-robustness test (acceptance criterion of ticket 0050): a closeout whose Run Status section value is written as 'Priority-launched run: archive ready.' (with the launch-target label prefix AND a trailing period) must still normalize to 'archive ready' and yield disposition='archive-confirmation' / status='awaiting-archive-confirmation'. This guards normalizeCloseoutRunStatusLine so we never regress the run_224 'Priority-launched run: archive ready.' validator failure.

MUST NOT BREAK: the full packages/core suite stays green. Do not weaken any unrelated assertion to make these pass; if a test must change, change it to assert the correct NEW behavior, not to silence it.

EXIT CRITERION (run it and paste output): `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` exits 0 with no failures. Also run `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core exec tsc --noEmit` (or the package typecheck) and confirm it passes. Do NOT run git or commit — the runner commits after I verify. Report the exact commands, exit codes, and the diff you made.

data.task:
Ticket 0050 atom 2 of ~3: prove the DAEMON archive-confirmation path with tests, and confirm the uncommitted daemon code (launcher.ts requestArchiveConfirmation + routes.ts POST /runs/:id/archive-confirmation + the runActions status-feed projection) is sound. The code is already written but UNVERIFIED and has NO test coverage.

WRITE-SCOPE: packages/daemon/tests/** (add the tests here, following the existing patterns in packages/daemon/tests/mutations.test.ts and authoring-play.test.ts). You MAY edit packages/daemon/src/** ONLY to fix a real wiring bug a test exposes — if you do, explain it; do not refactor. Do NOT touch packages/core or packages/personas this atom.

CONTEXT (already implemented, do not rewrite): requestArchiveConfirmation (packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts ~line 1562) handles POST /runs/:id/archive-confirmation (routes.ts ~line 945). On confirmation==='archive' (case/space-insensitive) it dispatches the archive-priority authoring Play for the run's priorityId with archiveActor 'founder', sets run status 'completed', and records archive-confirmation-received + archive-confirmation-archived events. On any other confirmation it records archive-confirmation-declined and returns {archived:false} WITHOUT archiving. It returns 409 for ticket/playbook-launched runs and for runs not in 'awaiting-archive-confirmation' (unless a recorded wrap-disposition action says otherwise via hasArchiveConfirmationAction). routes.ts runActions (~line 538) surfaces the archive-priority-confirmation action in the run's status feed only when run.status==='awaiting-archive-confirmation' and the wrap event carries the action.

TESTS TO ADD (cover the run_224 case end-to-end through the daemon route, mirroring how mutations.test.ts:~1695 already tests the direct archive-priority endpoint and asserts order.json pruning):
1) Founder confirms archive: a priority-launched run set to status 'awaiting-archive-confirmation' (with a recorded wrap-disposition event carrying the archive-priority-confirmation action) -> POST /runs/<id>/archive-confirmation {confirmation:'archive'} archives the priority THROUGH the archive-priority Play lane: the priority file is moved under the archive convention AND its id is pruned from cocoder/priorities/order.json AND the response has archived:true AND run status becomes 'completed' AND an archive-confirmation-archived event is recorded. Assert order.json no longer contains the id (this is the ticket's explicit 'prunes order.json' requirement).
2) Founder declines / says anything other than archive: same setup, POST {confirmation:'keep'} (or 'no') -> response archived:false, an archive-confirmation-declined event is recorded, the priority file is STILL live (not moved), order.json STILL contains the id, run status is unchanged, and NO archive-priority Play dispatch / commit happened. This is the 'saying anything other than archive keeps the priority live and does not silently archive' acceptance criterion.
3) Guards: a ticket-launched run (ticketId set) -> 409; a run that is not awaiting-archive-confirmation and has no recorded archive action -> 409; a request with a missing/empty confirmation field -> 400.
4) Status-feed projection: assert runActions / the run status feed exposes the {type:'archive-priority-confirmation', endpoint:'/runs/<id>/archive-confirmation', confirmWith:'archive'} action when the run is awaiting-archive-confirmation with the recorded action, and exposes NO such action once the run status is e.g. 'completed' or 'running'. (Place this where the existing status-feed/runActions behavior is tested; if there is no such test yet, add a focused one.)

MUST NOT BREAK: the full packages/daemon suite stays green. Reuse existing test fixtures/helpers (the temp cocoder home, store, seeded priority + order.json) rather than inventing new harness.

EXIT CRITERION (run and paste output): `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test` exits 0 and `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon typecheck` exits 0. Do NOT run git or commit. Report the exact commands, exit codes, the new test names, and any src wiring bug you had to fix.

data.task:
Ticket 0050 atom 3 of 3 (final): make the FOUNDER-FACING closeout route archive confirmation through the in-context action, and close the one test gap. Two deliverables.

WRITE-SCOPE: packages/personas/base/plays/wrap-up.md and packages/daemon/tests/oz-chat.test.ts ONLY. Do NOT touch any other file.

=== DELIVERABLE 1: wrap-up Play guidance (packages/personas/base/plays/wrap-up.md) ===
This is base Surface-A governance — edit PROSE only; do NOT restructure the fenced founder-closeout contract. Hard constraints (a runner.test.ts contract parser reads this fence): keep every `**Section**` heading exactly as-is; keep the two run-status vocabulary lines exactly `Priority-launched run: continue | blocked | archive ready.` and `Ticket-launched run: needs another run | closed | needs closing | blocked.`; keep the final line exactly `I'm standing by...`. You may rewrite the descriptive sentences under those headings and the numbered steps above the fence.

The problem (ticket 0050): when a priority wrap is `archive ready`, the closeout currently hands the founder a manual CLI/tool recipe, and Oscar tends to put the archive ask in 'Founder Decision Needed'. With the now-shipped runtime, a non-None Founder Decision Needed makes the run resolve to generic awaiting-founder and SUPPRESSES the first-class archive-confirmation action. The Play must prevent that.

Required guidance changes (preserve meaning of everything else):
1) In step 3 (the lifecycle-disposition step) and in the in-fence description of `archive ready`: state that an archive-ready PRIORITY wrap leaves the run in a first-class archive-confirmation state and the founder confirms IN CONTEXT — by replying `archive` (or `archive <runId>` in Oz chat), which routes through the single archive-priority Play lane. Never self-archive. The CLI `pnpm --dir <install-root> exec cocoder oz archive-priority <priorityId>` is a FALLBACK only, not the primary instruction.
2) In the 'Founder Decision Needed' in-fence description: add that for an archive-ready PRIORITY wrap this MUST be `None` — the archive confirmation is the run's first-class action, not a prose decision listed here; listing it here would suppress the archive-confirmation action. (Leave the ticket `needs closing` rule intact.)
3) In 'Teardown Readiness' (in-fence): for an archive-ready wrap, the standing-by line should convey that the run is standing by with a one-step archive confirmation (reply `archive` / `archive <runId>`) that archives through the governed lane; teardown still requires an explicit founder request. Keep the final `I'm standing by...` line unchanged.
4) Keep the existing rule that 'Recommended Next Step' must NOT name archive confirmation (it points at the next priority/ticket to launch) — do not change that.

=== DELIVERABLE 2: positive Oz-chat archive command test (packages/daemon/tests/oz-chat.test.ts) ===
The `archive <runId>` chat command (oz-chat.ts: parseOzCommand maps verb `archive`/`confirm-archive` to {kind:'archive-confirmation', confirmation:'archive'}; executeOzCommand dispatches requestArchiveConfirmation; archiveConfirmationReply formats the reply) currently has NO positive coverage — only mock wiring. Add focused tests following the existing oz-chat.test.ts patterns:
  a) parseOzCommand('archive run_7') returns {kind:'archive-confirmation', runId:'run_7', confirmation:'archive'} (and the malformed/zero-arg form is an unknown command).
  b) handleOzMessage/executeOzCommand for `archive <runId>` dispatches requestArchiveConfirmation and, on an archived:true result, returns an ok reply with command 'archive-confirmation' and an action carrying the runId/priorityId; on an archived:false (declined) result, returns a reply stating the priority remains live. Use the mockOps/unexpected() harness already in the file.

MUST NOT BREAK: the founder-closeout contract parser and all Play/persona/daemon suites stay green.

EXIT CRITERION (run all and paste output+exit codes): `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` (contract parser + wrap derivations), `pnpm --filter @cocoder/personas test` (base Play/persona checks), and `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test` (the new chat tests) all exit 0; plus `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon typecheck` exits 0. Do NOT run git or commit. Report exact commands, exit codes, the new test names, and the wrap-up.md diff.
